There is an item kiosk to the right of the entrance to the emporium proper, lets you move items in and out of local storage more easily than the cargo elevator, though doesn't count towards turn-ins, the way I prefer to turn in items especially in bulk is carry it in a stor*all box, store the box either via the cargo elevator or drag it into the item kiosk, then unload the box, go to the cargo elevator kiosk and send what you're turning in up the cargo elevator, and then down it to turn in. Can make stacks of the items in the item kiosk UI and then move the stacks to the cargo elevator in the cargo elevator kiosk.

Wikelo has 3 missions he trades you 1 of 4 ships, with a custom gold & pearl paint and components, Favors are traded for 50x Council / MG Scrip from the Foxwell / HH patrol / ambush, and Hunt The Polaris.
⭐= Component not sold in-game. ⚖️ = Weighting, higher number is higher odds, i.e. Intrepid is the most likely reward from "Ship Surprise" the 3 tiers of mission all have 4 possible rewards each.

The initial mission that unlocks Wikelo's other missions is called "Arrive To System" in 4.1.1 it's been adjusted, it now needs 3x Tundra Kopion Horn, 1x Snaggle Stick (Smoke Daddy) and 1x Get Up Coffee (Milk) click the links to see where you can buy them, except Tundra Kopion Horns, which I've detailed how to get here: Tundra Kopion Habitat all 3 can be found at microTech (surface, spaceport, Port Tressler).

4.1.1 Cost: 20x Favors = (1000x MG Scrip / Council Scrip) 
4.2 Cost: 10x Favors
Odds seem highest at Dasi (near Hurston)
Odds seems highest at Kinga (near microTech)

4.2 Cost: 30x Favors, 10x Carinite, 15x Jaclium, 20x Saldynium (Hathor), 30x Ace Interceptor Helmet
You get one ship of the 4 possible, if you want a specific ship, you'll need to run the mission several times probably, especially if the ⚖️ number is lower.

It's random which ship you get, but there is some weighting denoted by ⚖️ higher number = higher chance, the components aren't attached to the ships (the ships come with stock components), instead the components appear in local storage at the emporium the "mission" got handed in at, and you can retrieve them using the cargo elevator to load into a ship's slots or bring with you.
